Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

cfs_kallsyms_lookup_name() is too effective now... we used to not call apply_workqueue_attrs() new kernels (RHEL 9.5) because we couldn't access the symbol. Now that we can, we crash.

parents.subject: LU-18243 lfs: Add --output and --no-header options to lfs df command

commit.subject: LU-18687 compat: improve cfs_kallsyms_lookup_name()
commit.message:
LU-18687 compat: improve cfs_kallsyms_lookup_name()

We can (ab)use kprobes to lookup kallsyms_lookup_name()
to enable us more kernel symbols. This can greatly simplify
Lustre compatibiliy code. lustre_compat/symbols.c will
serve as the central location for all of Lustre's symbol
re-exports.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Change-Id: I72b23c15c49f6a974187c96b09c0a701a96d5a92
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/59813
Reviewed-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
Reviewed-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
